posts.author: mohel
posts.title: The Stasi’s surveillance network spiralled out into every aspect of daily life. Among an estimated 274,000 employees were at least 174,000 informants, which would have been about 2.5% of the working population

link_metadata.description: Four decades of domination over almost all aspects of life in East Germany came to an abrupt halt exactly 25 years ago today. On 31 March 1990, one of the most intrusive surveillance organisations in human history, the Ministry for State Security, more infamously known as the Stasi, was dissolved.
link_metadata.title: Lessons from the Stasi – A cautionary tale on mass surveillance

posts.author: ChinchillaCapitalism
posts.title: The left stole another batch of House seats. Now is the time for righteous anger not limp wristed depression. The fight has escalated not ended

posts.raw_content:
Yeah, probably not the Trump you voted for, because you're probably a groyper more than you are MAGA. The promise "no new wars" is always conditional, it can never be an unconditional promise because you can't control everything in the world. It's possible that Trumps escelation with Kim Jong Un could have resulted in war, but it didn't. Trump has always been willing to risk war in the name of peace. "No new wars" means my platform is stability and not war, whereas warhawks and democrats their platform is forever wars.

The same Trump that said "no new wars" also took out Isis first day he got in office. That could be seen as an "act of aggression" but it was an act of peace.

Iran has been at war with us, our allies, and the world in general since the 1970s. Taking out Iran regime isn't starting a new war, it's ending one that started in the 70s that others haven't dealt with. Iran has declared it's violent desire to destroy America and Israel since the 1970s. And I don't care about your opinions on Israel. If Iran has made England or Canada it's mortal enemy since the 1970s the US would also get involved, just like we got involved with Ukraine Russia. And I'm pro Russia btw, so just because we get involved doesn't mean I side with the one we're siding with. With Ukraine Russia, I side with Russia. With Iran Israel, I side with Israel. But the point is our actions are not inconsistent. We would aid England and other European "allies" despite how much they crap on us if Iran was building nuclear stockpiles and escalating threats towards Germany or Greece, to the degree that we're part of NATO. If you don't like that, then your argument should be to leave or rearrange allies, not to have different standards of responses because you personally hate Israel. 

I hate NATO, so my argument is to ally with Russia. But since NATO is our current ally, it is consistent that we aid allied nations. That is the foreign policy of all nations everywhere, and yet with Trump and Israel with Iran, suddenly it's proof of him being "Israel first" and "owned by Israel".

Again, Iran is not a "new war". They have been a thorn in the world's side since their regime change in the 1970s. Just as USSR cold war had hot and cold moments, we're experiencing a hot moment. 

But no new wars never meant no military action and boldness. Part of Trump's entire promise was to make the military strong again and have peace through strength. He declared the department of defense the department of war for goodness sakes and got out the wokeness in the military.

He also, during his campaign declared regaining the Panama Canal. It's very likely that in the reclaiming of the Panama Canal it could lead to military conflict. Doesn't this contradict "no new wars" promise if there's military clash over the Panama Canal? No, because "no new wars" meant I will try to establish peace, and unlike Obama who was weak, sometimes to establish peace, you have to drop the hammer. No new wars means I will not bow to the military lobbying and sacrifice people for evil profit like warhawks do. It doesn't mean "I will never take military action where it is called for". That would be a weak leader, not a strong one. No new wars has always been understood as "to the degree it's possible and pertinant, I will not engage in needless conflict" but avoiding necessary conflict is cowardice. Iran has been a known threat for 50 years. And unlike Kim Jong Un who was able to be talked to and reasoned with, Iran is not reasonable at all and if you can't reason with the carrot, you give them the stick. Iran should have changed their tune when their uraniam plants were bombed. But they didn't. They should have then changed their tune when their leader was taken out, but they didn't. If war and death is the only thing a people understand, then that's what will be brought to their doorstep. Backing away is weakness and cowardice, NOT a principled keeping of a promise of no new wars. Once again, Iran is not a new war, it's a cold to hot war that has been going on since the 1970s. We're year 1 of Trumps presidency and people are acting like he's got 2 months left in office.

When redditors and groypers look the same, the one thing you can probably assume is they're not MAGA.

MAGA encompasses a lot of things, and it predated Trump, and will be around after Trump. MAGA was just the term given to the resentment Americans have been feeling about many things in America. If you think MAGA is primarily about how America interacts with Israel than you're a groyper or a redditor, you're not MAGA as the American way of life is far more encompassing. 

And people think that Trump can and only does one thing at a time? When has that ever been the case? As this conflict with Iran has happened he recently is also about to declare an executive order that banks must verify citizenship, thus creating the incentive for many illegals to self deport. If you can't bank in the country, and your reason here is primarily financial, the decision to self deport becomes a much more attractive option. How is Trump able to do this while also having a conflict in Iran? Almost like part of being a president is juggling many things at once and delegating many responsibilities and actions.

posts.title: "tHiS nOt ThE tRuMp wE vOtEd FoR"
